title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Oil Prices Drive Mixed Sentiment
subtitle: Wednesday, March 04, 2026

items.headline: Global Markets Exhibit Volatility Amid Escalating Middle East Conflict and Rising Crude Oil Prices
items.summary: Equity markets are experiencing a mixed trading session as investors closely monitor intensifying geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which have driven crude oil prices higher and fueled inflation concerns. While major U.S. indices show slight intraday movements, a cautious sentiment prevails with Asian markets registering significant declines.

items.headline: Moderna Soars on Patent Settlement
items.summary: Moderna ($MRNA) saw its shares climb 8.9% following a reported $2.25 billion settlement with Arbutus and Roivant, effectively clearing its pipeline with Merck.

items.headline: Ross Stores Rises on Strong Earnings and Buyback
items.summary: Ross Stores ($ROST) gained 6.6% in pre-market trading after reporting earnings that surpassed analyst estimates and announcing a new share buyback authorization.

items.headline: Bharti Airtel Leads Indian Market Gains
items.summary: Bharti Airtel Ltd. (N/A) closed up 1.93%, showcasing strength in the Indian market.

items.headline: Tata Steel Plunges Amid Broader Market Sell-off
items.summary: Tata Steel's shares experienced a significant decline, closing down 7.08% and weighing heavily on the NIFTY50 index, as Indian markets faced a broad sell-off.

items.headline: Mahanagar Gas Falls on Geopolitical Gas Supply Concerns
items.summary: Mahanagar Gas saw a sharp drop of 9.63% as gas stocks were hit hard by reports of Qatar shutting liquefied natural gas (LNG) production following Iranian strikes, impacting transit through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Petronet LNG Shares Decline as Strait of Hormuz Transit Halted
items.summary: Petronet LNG's stock fell 9% amidst concerns over gas supply disruptions, as vessels were reportedly unable to safely transit the Strait of Hormuz to reach Ras Laffan.

items.headline: Energy and Materials Show Strength, Technology Recovers Intraday; Metal Sector Leads Losses
items.summary: While Energy and Materials sectors have maintained strong relative uptrends earlier this week, the Technology sector demonstrated signs of recovery during Wednesday's live trading. However, metal stocks led overall losses, declining 4%. Financials and Consumer Discretionary continued to lag.

items.headline: Fed's March Meeting Looms; Inflation and Job Data in Focus
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's next FOMC meeting is scheduled for March 17-18, with market participants anticipating no change to the federal funds rate of 3.50%-3.75%. Recent January data showed inflation at 2.9% (above target) and unemployment at 4.3%. The upcoming ADP private payrolls report and inflation reading on March 11 are keenly awaited.

items.headline: Mortgage Rates Hold Steady Ahead of Key Economic Reports
items.summary: As of March 4, 2026, the average mortgage interest rate for a 30-year term stands at 5.87%, while a 15-year term is at 5.37%. Lenders are in a holding pattern awaiting upcoming unemployment and inflation reports later this month, which could influence future rate movements.

items.headline: MDA Space Reports Record Q4 2025 Results and Positive 2026 Outlook
items.summary: MDA Space ($MDA) announced record revenues of $499 million (up 44% YoY) and adjusted EBITDA of $96 million (up 36% YoY) for Q4 2025. The company also introduced a positive financial outlook for 2026, projecting revenues between $1.7 billion and $1.9 billion.

items.headline: Abercrombie & Fitch, Broadcom, Rigetti, Okta Among Companies Reporting Earnings Today
items.summary: Several major companies, including Abercrombie & Fitch ($ANF), Broadcom ($AVGO), Rigetti ($RGTI), and Okta ($OKTA), are scheduled to release their earnings reports today, March 4th. Investors will be watching for financial updates and forward guidance.

items.headline: USMCA Review in 2026: A Decisive Year for North American Competitiveness
items.summary: The year 2026 is poised to be decisive for North America, with the USMCA review providing an opportunity to enhance joint resilience and boost shared competitiveness, particularly in addressing supply chain weaknesses like in the semiconductor industry.
